task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical evaluation or encounter has been opened for the patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health interface showing a patient record, evaluation, or encounter specifically for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the patient's vitals are correctly recorded in the clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Clinical evaluation form showing Temperature as 38.5 (or 38.5°C), Heart Rate as 110 (bpm), and Systolic BP as 100 (mmHg).

task_completion.description: Verify that an occupational heat illness diagnosis is recorded using the correct ICD-10 code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Diagnosis or conditions section showing an ICD-10 code starting with 'T67' (e.g., T67.0, T67.x) associated with the patient's evaluation.

task_completion.description: Verify that a prescription for intravenous hydration therapy has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Medication or prescription section showing an order containing keywords like 'Sodium Chloride', 'Saline', 'Ringer', or 'Hartmann'.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two metabolic laboratory tests have been ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Laboratory or test request section showing at least 2 distinct lab test orders (e.g., metabolic panel, electrolytes, BUN/Creatinine) for the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify that a follow-up appointment is scheduled in 2 to 7 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Appointments section showing a scheduled appointment for John Zenon with a date that is 2 to 7 days after the current system date, ideally noting renal function reassessment.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client windows, menus, and forms, not terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually entered the data into the appropriate fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data (vitals, diagnosis, prescriptions) should be visible inside the correct GNU Health form fields, not just typed into a notepad or a single free-text notes field.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply copy-paste the task description into a generic text box.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs should be distributed across the structured fields (e.g., specific boxes for Temperature, HR, BP, ICD-10 search) rather than a single block of text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is John Zenon. The diagnosis must use an ICD-10 code starting with 'T67'. The hydration prescription must include keywords like 'sodium', 'chloride', 'saline', or 'ringer'. At least 2 new lab test orders must be present. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled exactly between 2 and 7 days from the current date.
